page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores based on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture (AWS Neoverse-V2) running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local NVMe storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. There are no integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. The r8gb.12xlarge achieves top-tier results in large-block memory bandwidth tests, with read speeds of 434.99 GB/sec and write speeds of 216.28 GB/sec. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark database operations also rank in the top tier. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing scoring in the average tier. The system demonstrates strong capabilities in Redis SET operations, reaching 101.9 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 99.24 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv9 architecture offers high memory density and high-throughput networking without the overhead of simultaneous multithreading, as each of the 48 vCPUs maps to a dedicated physical core. However, the lack of local instance storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for disk-heavy operations. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server instance powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor family running at 2.8 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in large-block memory bandwidth, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and PassMark database operations, alongside strong Redis SET throughput of 101.9 million operations per second. Single-core performance remains average. Given its high memory-to-core ratio and dedicated physical cores, the r8gb.12xlarge is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving, offering a cost-efficient profile for memory-dense enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores and high-bandwidth memory for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARMv9 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmarks
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and high-throughput web serving
